notes: KNOWN FAILING (pid_bind + teardown phases only) — the headless error-abort run completes in ~37ms, before the daemon's PID scanner ticks, so no pid_discovered binds and no process_exited is captured for the rollout session; the load-bearing turn_aborted phase (working→ready via the error epilogue, never enters waiting) DOES match. INHERENT fast-error-exit observability gap, NOT a spec error and NOT a rebase: a live-REPL re-record that would keep the process alive is impossible because pi rejects `/model <bogus>` synchronously (opens a picker, emits no turn error) — the bogus-model abort path is reachable only via the CLI --model flag, which exits instantly. Re-record and clear this flag if the daemon gains sub-100ms PID binding. Original spec rationale follows. A pi turn aborts on a DETERMINISTIC, non-faked, NON-retryable provider error: the headless driver launches `pi --print -p <prompt>` against a bogus model id (IRRLICHT_PI_MODEL=<provider>/<unsupported-model>), so the provider returns an 'unsupported model' error whose message does NOT match pi's retry regex (_isRetryableError) — pi does not retry, persists a single assistant message with stopReason='error', and exits non-zero. DELIBERATELY non-retryable: a 5xx/rate-limit class message would be silently retried up to retry.maxRetries and is non-deterministic, unfit for a committed fixture. The daemon-observable arc is the SAME shape as basic-turn (one rollout session, ready→working→ready, single process_exited, final ready) but the working→ready settle is driven by the ERROR epilogue rather than a stopReason=stop: pi's parser maps every non-'stop' stopReason (incl. 'error') to EventType='assistant', and IsAgentDone()'s assistant-fallback (no open tool call) flips working→ready via classifier rule 2b ('agent finished turn → ready'). The turn does NOT stick in working. NOTE on the scenario's 'sweep' primitive: there is no working-state idle sweep in the daemon today (PR #106 removed the stale-tool timer); pi reaches the same user-observable end-state (resolves to ready, no stick in working) via the error epilogue, so this fixture asserts that end-state, NOT an actual sweep transition. HEADLESS shape (same as pi/basic-turn): pi writes one JSONL rollout file per invocation on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy), so the daemon's first scanner tick keys the real UUID transcript — a SINGLE rollout session, no separately-matchable proc-<PID> pre-session phase. HOW runs differ: the exact provider errorMessage wording, sub-second timing, and whether any mid-turn assistant burst precedes the error all vary; structurally stable is one ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id that never enters waiting, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
The model call aborts with a non-retryable provider error → the turn resolves back to ready; state does not stick in working and never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
Headless pi exits non-zero after the aborted turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
